site.title: Celestia Island
site.description: An independent open-source organization exploring the frontiers of systems engineering

group.toolsDesc: Allowing agents to engage with the real world

entelecheia.name: Entelecheia
entelecheia.tagline: Multi-Agent Platform for Industrial AI Control
entelecheia.description: An exec-only microkernel multi-agent platform built for safety-critical industrial control. The LLM sees only a handful of primitives — all real work runs inside the IEPL TypeScript pipeline, dispatching to a large MCP tool surface across many agents, with cross-vendor protocol support (Modbus, S7comm, OPC UA) and multi-layer safety gates.

shittim.name: Shittim Chest
shittim.description: The interactive face of Entelecheia — a streaming chat interface to converse with AI agents, a control panel to configure systems and manage permissions, plus desktop and mobile apps. Everything the user sees and touches.

tairitsu.name: Tairitsu
tairitsu.description: Build complete web applications that target WASI — a single, portable runtime for both frontend and backend. Write your UI and server logic in Rust, compile to WASI components, and deploy to browser, server, or edge. One runtime, one language, one codebase.

hikari.name: Hikari
hikari.description: A UI component library purpose-built for WASI. Polished, ready-made components that run on the same WASI runtime as your backend — no wasm-bindgen, no JavaScript glue. Build interfaces once, render them anywhere.

aoba.name: Aoba
aoba.tagline: Multi-Protocol Debugging & Simulation for Modbus RTU
aoba.description: Inspect, test, and simulate Modbus industrial devices right from your terminal. Connect to real hardware, spin up virtual devices for testing, and bridge data between protocols — an indispensable companion for automation engineers on the factory floor and in CI pipelines.

kirino.name: Kirino
kirino.tagline: Zero-trust auth & RBAC, with trust scoring
kirino.description: A fully generic, trait-based authentication and authorization framework for Rust. Complete RBAC (RBAC0/1/2) implementing ANSI INCITS 359-2004, plus a dynamic authorization layer with trust scoring, anomaly detection, and DO-178C-inspired autonomy levels (L0–L4).

ratatui-markdown.name: Ratatui Markdown
ratatui-markdown.tagline: Rich markdown and diagrams for terminal apps
ratatui-markdown.description: Markdown rendering, syntax highlighting, Mermaid diagrams, collapsible JSON/TOML trees, and a hybrid scroll system — all built on ratatui. Feature-gated for selective inclusion in any TUI application.

yuuka.name: Yuuka
yuuka.tagline: Define deeply nested types in one sweep
yuuka.description: A proc-macro library that generates complex, deeply nested structs and enums from a compact DSL. Inline types are automatically flattened, derived, and wired up with serde — write one macro invocation, get an entire type hierarchy.

ichika.name: Ichika
ichika.description: Declaratively define multi-stage processing pipelines from closures. The pipe! macro generates all thread wiring and channel plumbing automatically — supporting mixed sync/async steps, branching, retry policies, and graceful shutdown.

hifumi.name: Hifumi
hifumi.tagline: Version your structs, migrate your data
hifumi.description: A serialization library for migrating data between struct versions. Annotate your types with version attributes and migration rules — Hifumi auto-generates all older-version structs and From impls, plus a CLI to diff structs across git history.

noa.name: Noa
noa.description: Per-agent workspace isolation, append-only JSONL logs, snapshot-based history, and full git protocol compatibility — version control designed from the ground up for AI agents that branch constantly and rewrite history.

evernight.name: Evernight
evernight.description: Screen streaming, SSH/VNC/RDP, hardware telemetry, and industrial protocols — Modbus, S7comm, OPC UA, CAN, EtherCAT. The layer that connects cloud AI agents to physical devices and orchestrates containers and VMs at the edge.

aris.name: ARIS
aris.tagline: Embedded OS for industrial IoT gateways
aris.description: The embedded OS and firmware for the Entelecheia industrial IoT gateway. It runs evernight on ARM/RISC-V edge devices, bridging the protocol broker to physical hardware — PLCs, sensors, valves — through a minimal, secure kernel layer.

kei.name: KEI
kei.tagline: Asterinas ARM64 fork for industrial IoT gateways
kei.description: An independent fork of asterinas with ARM64 support and board support packages for industrial IoT gateways. It provides the kei-kernel.bin consumed by aris — absorbing upstream changes on its own schedule rather than tracking them as a branch.

lagrange.name: Lagrange
lagrange.description: Turn a folder of Markdown into a static, multilingual documentation site — WASI-rendered, with a built-in language switcher and root redirect. No JavaScript framework, no mdBook, no Node toolchain. Lagrange builds its own documentation.

kou.name: kou
kou.description: PTY management, a VT100/ANSI screen emulator, and glyph-rendering screen output in one library and CLI. The vtty core extracted from the tairitsu packager — a real CSI/SGR-honouring screen, not a bare PTY wrapper.

malkuth.name: Malkuth
malkuth.description: Graceful shutdown, health probes, coordination locks, and rolling updates for long-running Rust programs. Pluggable transport (JSON-RPC over TCP/WebSocket/IPC), supervised workers, and a watchdog CLI that wraps any program with file watching and a sticky reverse proxy.

seia.name: seia
seia.tagline: Multi-engine web search library and CLI
seia.description: A unified interface to query diverse search backends. Engines that need no authentication work out of the box with zero configuration — a library and CLI for routing one query across many providers and merging the results.

shirabe.name: shirabe
shirabe.description: A lightweight, Rust-native browser automation library and debug server. It drives any CDP-speaking browser — Chrome, Chromium, Edge — through one hand-rolled engine, fetching and pinning a Chrome for Testing build so you never install a browser by hand.
